articles.headline: India Allows Iranian Warship IRIS Lavan to Dock in Kochi on Humanitarian Grounds Amid Regional Tensions
articles.explainer:
• The Indian government granted permission for the Iranian Navy vessel IRIS Lavan to dock at Kochi port, citing humanitarian grounds during the ongoing 2026 Iran war.
• This decision underscores India's commitment to humanitarian principles and its historically neutral stance in regional conflicts, despite geopolitical complexities.
• The move highlights India's strategic autonomy in foreign policy, balancing international relations with its national interests and maritime security concerns.
• The docking facilitates necessary repairs and replenishment for the vessel, which was reportedly facing distress, without implying political endorsement of any party in the conflict.
• The incident brings into focus India's role as a responsible maritime power and a provider of humanitarian assistance in the Indian Ocean region.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
prelimsPoints: IRIS Lavan is an Iranian Navy vessel.
Kochi is a major port on India's southwestern coast.
The docking was allowed on 'humanitarian grounds'.
Context is the '2026 Iran war'.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's balanced approach to regional conflicts and its commitment to humanitarian diplomacy.
Analyze the implications of such decisions on India's strategic autonomy and its relations with other global powers.
Evaluate India's role as a net security provider and humanitarian responder in the Indian Ocean Region.
Examine the challenges of maintaining neutrality amidst evolving geopolitical landscapes.
articles.imageDescription: An illustration of an Iranian naval vessel docked at a port, with the Indian coastline in the background.
articles.trivia: Kochi, historically known as Cochin, was a pivotal spice trade center and has been nicknamed the 'Queen of the Arabian Sea'.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: International Relations
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Impact of global events on India

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Data Protection Authority's Powers in Landmark Privacy Ruling
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India delivered a crucial verdict, affirming the extensive enforcement powers of the Data Protection Authority (DPA) established under the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023.
• The ruling validates DPA's authority to impose penalties, conduct investigations, and issue directives, significantly strengthening India's data governance and privacy framework.
• The court emphasized the importance of balancing individual right to privacy with legitimate state interests and the needs of a digital economy, setting a precedent for future data regulation.
• This judgment will have far-reaching implications for technology companies operating in India, mandating stricter compliance with data handling and processing norms.
• Experts suggest the ruling bolsters India's position as a robust regulatory environment for digital services, potentially influencing global data protection standards.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies
Right to Privacy
Information Technology
keyTerms: Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act)
prelimsPoints: The Supreme Court upheld the powers of the Data Protection Authority (DPA).
The DPA was established under the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023.
The ruling pertains to individual right to privacy and data governance.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the Supreme Court's ruling on the DPA's powers for India's digital economy and individual rights.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in implementing a robust data protection regime in a rapidly digitizing society.
Evaluate the role of statutory bodies like the DPA in upholding constitutional principles and ensuring fair digital practices.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel resting on law books with binary code flowing in the background, symbolizing digital justice.
articles.trivia: India's Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, is one of the most comprehensive data protection laws globally, drawing parallels with the EU's GDPR.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies

articles.headline: Government Unveils 'Atmanirbhar Bharat 2.0' with Focus on Green Manufacturing and Export Boost
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government launched the second phase of the 'Atmanirbhar Bharat Abhiyan', shifting its focus towards sustainable and green manufacturing practices.
• Key initiatives include substantial incentives for industries adopting renewable energy sources, producing electric vehicle components, and engaging in circular economy models.
• The policy aims to boost India's export capabilities in emerging sectors like green technology, advanced materials, and sustainable agriculture products.
• It seeks to create a resilient global supply chain, positioning India as a reliable manufacturing hub for environmentally conscious products.
• This phase emphasizes skill development and R&D in green technologies to foster innovation and create new employment opportunities.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it
Government Budgeting
Environmental pollution and degradation
prelimsPoints: 'Atmanirbhar Bharat 2.0' focuses on green manufacturing and export boost.
Incentives target renewable energy, EV components, and circular economy models.
Aims to position India as a sustainable manufacturing hub.
mainsPoints: Analyze the potential impact of 'Atmanirbhar Bharat 2.0' on India's economic growth, environmental sustainability, and global trade position.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in transitioning towards a green manufacturing economy in India.
Evaluate how this policy aligns with India's international climate commitments and sustainable development goals.
articles.imageDescription: A factory emitting green smoke, symbolizing eco-friendly industrial growth, with the Indian flag subtly in the background.
articles.trivia: The original Atmanirbhar Bharat Abhiyan, launched in 2020, was a comprehensive economic package aimed at making India self-reliant across various sectors.

articles.headline: India Launches National Mission for Climate Resilient Agriculture (NMCRA)
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare officially launched the National Mission for Climate Resilient Agriculture (NMCRA), a flagship program to combat climate change impacts on farming.
• The mission focuses on developing and deploying climate-smart agricultural practices, including drought-resistant crop varieties, precision irrigation techniques, and soil health management.
• It aims to integrate traditional ecological knowledge with modern agricultural science to enhance the adaptive capacity of Indian farmers.
• NMCRA will establish a network of climate-resilient villages and promote sustainable land and water resource management across vulnerable regions.
• The initiative is crucial for ensuring long-term food security and improving farmers' livelihoods amidst increasing climate variability.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country
Storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints
Food processing and related industries in India
prelimsPoints: NMCRA stands for National Mission for Climate Resilient Agriculture.
It aims to combat climate change impacts on farming.
Focus areas include drought-resistant crops, precision irrigation, and soil health management.
mainsPoints: Discuss the necessity and strategic objectives of the National Mission for Climate Resilient Agriculture (NMCRA) in the context of India's agricultural challenges.
Analyze how NMCRA can contribute to food security, farmers' income, and environmental sustainability.
Evaluate the potential for integrating traditional farming wisdom with modern scientific approaches under this mission.
articles.imageDescription: A farmer working in a field with advanced irrigation systems and changing weather patterns in the sky, symbolizing climate resilience.
articles.trivia: India is highly vulnerable to climate change impacts on agriculture, with millions of farmers dependent on monsoon rains.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Prototype, Advancing Space Access
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully conducted a crucial test flight of its Reusable Launch Vehicle – Technology Demonstrator (RLV-TD) prototype, marking a significant milestone.
• This test involved a controlled landing experiment simulating the return of a space vehicle to Earth, a critical step towards developing a fully reusable two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) RLV.
• The successful demonstration aims to drastically reduce the cost of space launches, making space access more affordable and frequent for commercial and scientific missions.
• The RLV program is central to ISRO's long-term strategy for future space exploration, including human spaceflight and setting up space stations.
• India's indigenous development of RLV technology positions it among a select few nations with such advanced capabilities, boosting its competitiveness in the global space market.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Space
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: ISRO successfully tested its Reusable Launch Vehicle – Technology Demonstrator (RLV-TD) prototype.
The test involved a controlled landing experiment.
The RLV program aims to reduce space launch costs.
India is among a few nations developing RLV technology.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) program for India's future in space exploration and the global space market.
Analyze how cost-effective space access through RLV technology can democratize space and foster innovation.
Evaluate the technological challenges and breakthroughs involved in developing indigenous RLV capabilities.
articles.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of ISRO's RLV-TD descending through the atmosphere towards a landing strip.
articles.trivia: ISRO's RLV-TD program is similar in concept to NASA's Space Shuttle and SpaceX's Falcon 9 reusable rockets, aiming for significant cost reductions.

articles.headline: India-ASEAN Summit Concludes with Focus on Digital Economy and Maritime Security Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-ASEAN Summit concluded with significant agreements aimed at deepening cooperation in the digital economy and strengthening maritime security across the Indo-Pacific.
• Leaders endorsed a 'Digital Partnership Framework' to facilitate cross-border digital payments, data sharing protocols, and collaboration on emerging technologies like AI and blockchain.
• In maritime security, joint declarations emphasized adherence to international law, freedom of navigation, and enhanced cooperation in counter-piracy, search and rescue, and disaster relief operations.
• The summit reinforced India's 'Act East Policy', highlighting ASEAN as a central pillar in India's vision for a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific region.
• Discussions also covered regional connectivity projects, trade facilitation, and collective efforts to address climate change and sustainable development goals.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
India and its neighbourhood- relations
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India-ASEAN Summit focused on digital economy and maritime security.
A 'Digital Partnership Framework' was endorsed.
Commitment to freedom of navigation and international law in Indo-Pacific was reiterated.
Reinforces India's 'Act East Policy'.
mainsPoints: Examine the significance of India-ASEAN cooperation in shaping the geopolitical and geoeconomic landscape of the Indo-Pacific region.
Discuss how the 'Digital Partnership Framework' can enhance regional economic integration and digital governance.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in strengthening maritime security cooperation between India and ASEAN member states.
articles.imageDescription: A handshake between representatives of India and ASEAN nations, with digital network lines and ships in the background.
articles.trivia: ASEAN (Association of Southeast Asian Nations) was founded in 1967 and comprises ten member states in Southeast Asia.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: International Relations
prelimsSyllabus.area: Important International institutions, agencies and fora

articles.headline: National Health Mission Expands Telemedicine Services to Remote Rural Areas
articles.explainer:
• The National Health Mission (NHM) announced a significant expansion of its telemedicine services, aiming to reach the most remote and underserved rural populations across India.
• This initiative leverages India's growing digital infrastructure, including widespread internet connectivity and mobile penetration, to provide remote medical consultations, diagnostics, and specialist referrals.
• The expansion seeks to bridge the critical gap in healthcare access, particularly addressing the shortage of doctors and medical facilities in rural areas.
• Training programs for community health workers (ASHAs and ANMs) are being intensified to facilitate teleconsultations and ensure effective last-mile delivery of healthcare.
• The move is expected to improve health outcomes, reduce out-of-pocket expenditure for rural families, and enhance the overall efficiency of the public health system.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: National Health Mission (NHM) expanded telemedicine services to rural areas.
Aims to bridge healthcare access gap and address doctor shortage.
Leverages digital infrastructure and trains community health workers.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of telemedicine in achieving universal healthcare access and equity in India, especially for rural populations.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities associated with scaling up digital health services in a diverse country like India.
Evaluate how the expansion of NHM's telemedicine services aligns with the broader goals of Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission.
articles.imageDescription: A doctor on a video call with a patient in a rural setting, symbolizing telemedicine bridging geographical gaps.
articles.trivia: The National Health Mission (NHM) was launched in 2013, subsuming the National Rural Health Mission (NRHM) and the National Urban Health Mission (NUHM).

articles.headline: New Counter-Terrorism Policy Framework Announced to Combat Hybrid Threats
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Home Affairs unveiled a comprehensive new Counter-Terrorism Policy Framework, specifically designed to address the evolving nature of 'hybrid threats'.
• The policy emphasizes enhanced intelligence gathering and sharing mechanisms, both domestically and internationally, to preempt and neutralize terror plots.
• It incorporates strategies to counter threats posed by cyber-terrorism, drone attacks, lone-wolf actors, and the online radicalization of youth.
• A key focus is on strengthening inter-agency coordination between various security forces, intelligence agencies, and state police departments.
• The framework also includes provisions for community engagement and de-radicalization programs to build societal resilience against extremist ideologies.
gsSubTopics: Linkages between development and spread of extremism
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security
Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
prelimsPoints: New Counter-Terrorism Policy Framework addresses 'hybrid threats'.
Focuses on cyber-terrorism, drone attacks, and online radicalization.
Emphasizes enhanced intelligence sharing and inter-agency coordination.
Includes community engagement and de-radicalization programs.
mainsPoints: Discuss the changing nature of terrorism and the emergence of 'hybrid threats' in India's internal security landscape.
Analyze the key features and strategic objectives of the new Counter-Terrorism Policy Framework in addressing these contemporary challenges.
Evaluate the effectiveness of multi-pronged approaches, including technological solutions, intelligence, and community engagement, in combating terrorism.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized graphic showing interconnected threats like drones, cyber symbols, and radicalization, converging on a map of India.
articles.trivia: Hybrid threats combine conventional, irregular, cyber, and information warfare tactics, often blurring the lines between state and non-state actors.

articles.headline: Major Boost to National Logistics Policy with New Integrated Freight Corridors
articles.explainer:
• The government announced a significant expansion of the National Logistics Policy (NLP) with the sanctioning of several new integrated freight corridors across key economic regions.
• These corridors aim to seamlessly connect manufacturing hubs, consumption centers, and ports through dedicated rail, road, and waterway networks, drastically improving freight movement efficiency.
• The initiative is projected to reduce logistics costs from the current high percentage of GDP, making Indian goods more competitive in domestic and international markets.
• It integrates advanced technologies like IoT, AI, and blockchain for real-time tracking, optimized route planning, and predictive maintenance of logistics infrastructure.
• The development is expected to create numerous employment opportunities, attract private investment in infrastructure, and accelerate economic growth.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Investment models
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
prelimsPoints: National Logistics Policy (NLP) boosted with new integrated freight corridors.
Aims to connect manufacturing hubs, consumption centers, and ports.
Expected to reduce logistics costs and improve competitiveness.
Integrates IoT, AI, and blockchain for logistics management.
mainsPoints: Discuss the critical role of robust logistics infrastructure in achieving India's economic growth and manufacturing aspirations.
Analyze how the new integrated freight corridors under NLP can address existing challenges in supply chain efficiency and cost reduction.
Evaluate the potential socio-economic benefits and environmental considerations of large-scale infrastructure projects like freight corridors.
articles.imageDescription: An aerial view of a complex network of railways, roads, and waterways converging, symbolizing integrated freight corridors.
articles.trivia: Logistics costs in India are significantly higher than global benchmarks, making improvements critical for boosting manufacturing competitiveness.

articles.headline: Ministry of Environment Designates Five New Ramsar Sites, Boosting Wetland Conservation Efforts
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change announced the addition of five new sites to India's list of Wetlands of International Importance under the Ramsar Convention.
• This designation significantly boosts India's efforts in wetland conservation, recognizing the ecological importance of these sites for biodiversity, water regulation, and local livelihoods.
• The newly added sites span diverse geographical regions and include a mix of natural and man-made wetlands, each supporting unique flora and fauna.
• The Ramsar Convention provides a framework for national action and international cooperation for the conservation and wise use of wetlands and their resources.
• The move emphasizes the need for integrated management plans to protect these fragile ecosystems from pollution, encroachment, and climate change impacts.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
Environmental impact assessment
prelimsPoints: Five new Ramsar Sites were designated in India.
Ramsar Convention is an international treaty for wetland conservation.
Wetlands are crucial for biodiversity, water regulation, and livelihoods.
The new sites cover diverse geographical regions.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological and socio-economic significance of wetlands and the importance of the Ramsar Convention in their conservation.
Analyze the challenges faced in wetland management in India and how the designation of new Ramsar sites contributes to addressing these challenges.
Suggest measures for effective implementation of wetland conservation strategies, including community participation and policy frameworks.
articles.imageDescription: A serene wetland landscape with various migratory birds, symbolizing biodiversity and conservation.
articles.trivia: The Ramsar Convention was signed in Ramsar, Iran, in 1971, making it one of the oldest intergovernmental environmental agreements.

articles.headline: Inter-State Council Meeting Discusses Fiscal Federalism and Cooperative Development
articles.explainer:
• The Inter-State Council convened a crucial meeting, bringing together Chief Ministers and Union Ministers to deliberate on matters pertaining to fiscal federalism and cooperative development.
• Discussions focused on equitable revenue sharing mechanisms, devolution of funds to states, and enhancing financial autonomy for state governments to foster balanced regional growth.
• The Council emphasized the need for greater collaboration between the Centre and States on key developmental projects, especially in critical sectors like infrastructure, education, and healthcare.
• Proposals for streamlining inter-state trade and investment policies were also considered, aiming to create a more unified national market.
• The meeting reinforced the spirit of cooperative federalism, seeking to resolve inter-state disputes and strengthen the institutional framework for Centre-State relations.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
prelimsPoints: Inter-State Council meeting discussed fiscal federalism and cooperative development.
Focused on revenue sharing, devolution of funds, and state financial autonomy.
Aimed at strengthening Centre-State relations and resolving disputes.
The Inter-State Council is established under Article 263 of the Constitution.
mainsPoints: Discuss the concept of fiscal federalism in India and the challenges in achieving equitable resource distribution between the Centre and States.
Analyze the role and significance of the Inter-State Council in promoting cooperative federalism and resolving inter-state disputes.
Suggest reforms to strengthen the institutional mechanisms for Centre-State cooperation and improve financial autonomy of states.
articles.imageDescription: A map of India with interconnected lines, symbolizing Centre-State cooperation, with government officials in a meeting setting.
articles.trivia: The Inter-State Council is a non-permanent constitutional body established under Article 263 of the Indian Constitution to facilitate coordination between states and the Centre.

articles.headline: Breakthrough in AI-Powered Drug Discovery for Neglected Tropical Diseases
articles.explainer:
• Indian research institutions, led by the Council of Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR), announced a significant breakthrough in using Artificial Intelligence (AI) for drug discovery.
• Researchers successfully identified several promising drug candidates for neglected tropical diseases (NTDs) by employing advanced AI algorithms to screen millions of compounds.
• This AI-driven approach drastically reduces the time and cost traditionally associated with drug development, offering hope for affordable treatments for diseases affecting vulnerable populations.
• The initiative highlights India's growing capabilities in leveraging cutting-edge technology for public health challenges and global health equity.
• Future plans include clinical trials for the identified compounds and collaboration with international pharmaceutical companies to accelerate the development and distribution of new drugs.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Health
prelimsPoints: Indian institutions achieved breakthrough in AI-powered drug discovery for Neglected Tropical Diseases (NTDs).
AI algorithms screened millions of compounds to identify drug candidates.
Aims to reduce time and cost of drug development.
CSIR is involved in this research.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of Artificial Intelligence in accelerating drug discovery and addressing global health challenges, particularly for neglected diseases.
Analyze how India's advancements in AI and biotechnology can contribute to global health equity and affordable healthcare solutions.
Evaluate the ethical considerations and regulatory frameworks required for AI-driven drug development and deployment.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized image of a human brain merging with a molecular structure, symbolizing AI's role in drug discovery.
articles.trivia: Neglected Tropical Diseases (NTDs) are a diverse group of communicable diseases that prevail in tropical and subtropical conditions in 149 countries and affect more than one billion people.

articles.headline: RBI Introduces New Regulatory Framework for Digital Banks and Fintech Lenders
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) unveiled a new comprehensive regulatory framework aimed at governing digital banks and other fintech lending platforms.
• The framework seeks to ensure financial stability, protect consumer interests, and promote responsible innovation in the rapidly expanding digital lending space.
• Key provisions include stricter capital adequacy norms for digital banks, enhanced data privacy requirements, and robust grievance redressal mechanisms for customers.
• The move is designed to mitigate risks associated with predatory lending practices, data breaches, and systemic vulnerabilities that could arise from unregulated digital financial services.
• It is expected to create a level playing field for traditional banks and fintech entities, fostering healthy competition while ensuring financial inclusion and market integrity.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it
Government Budgeting
Financial market
prelimsPoints: RBI introduced a new regulatory framework for digital banks and fintech lenders.
Aims for financial stability, consumer protection, and responsible innovation.
Includes capital adequacy norms, data privacy, and grievance redressal mechanisms.
Seeks to mitigate risks from predatory lending and data breaches.
mainsPoints: Discuss the rationale behind RBI's new regulatory framework for digital banks and fintech lenders in the context of India's evolving financial landscape.
Analyze the potential benefits and challenges of this framework for financial inclusion, innovation, and consumer protection.
Evaluate how a balanced regulatory approach can foster growth in the digital financial sector while safeguarding systemic stability.
articles.imageDescription: A digital padlock over a stack of coins and credit cards, symbolizing financial regulation in the digital age.
articles.trivia: The RBI had previously issued guidelines for digital lending in 2022, but this new framework is more comprehensive, specifically addressing digital banks.

articles.headline: UNESCO Recognizes Traditional Indian Textile Craft as Intangible Cultural Heritage
articles.explainer:
• The United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization (UNESCO) officially inscribed a prominent traditional Indian textile craft onto its Representative List of the Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity.
• This recognition highlights the intricate artistry, historical significance, and community-based knowledge systems associated with the craft, which has been passed down through generations.
• The designation is expected to provide a significant boost to the artisans involved, promoting their livelihoods, and attracting global attention to India's rich cultural heritage.
• It also underscores the urgent need for preservation efforts against modernization pressures and challenges in sustaining traditional skills.
• India's Ministry of Culture expressed commitment to developing comprehensive strategies for safeguarding and promoting this newly recognized heritage, including skill development and market linkages.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Salient features of Indian Society, Diversity of India.
prelimsPoints: UNESCO recognized a traditional Indian textile craft as Intangible Cultural Heritage.
Aims to boost artisans' livelihoods and promote cultural preservation.
Highlights intricate artistry and historical significance.
India has several elements on UNESCO's Intangible Cultural Heritage list.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of UNESCO's recognition of traditional Indian crafts for cultural preservation, economic development, and India's soft power.
Analyze the challenges faced by traditional artisans in India and propose strategies for sustaining and promoting intangible cultural heritage.
Evaluate the role of government policies and community initiatives in safeguarding traditional art forms against modernization and commercialization.
articles.imageDescription: A close-up of intricate traditional Indian textile patterns being woven by an artisan's hands.
articles.trivia: India has numerous elements on UNESCO's Intangible Cultural Heritage list, including Yoga, Kumbh Mela, and Novruz.

articles.headline: India Participates in Quad Maritime Security Dialogue, Emphasizing Rules-Based Order
articles.explainer:
• India participated in a high-level Quad Maritime Security Dialogue, reaffirming its commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific region based on a rules-based international order.
• Representatives from India, the United States, Japan, and Australia discussed enhanced cooperation in maritime domain awareness, counter-piracy operations, and disaster relief efforts.
• The dialogue focused on strengthening interoperability among member navies and sharing best practices for maintaining stability and security in critical sea lanes.
• India reiterated its stance on respecting international law, particularly the UN Convention on the Law of the Sea (UNCLOS), as the foundation for maritime governance.
• The Quad's growing focus on practical cooperation in maritime security underscores its evolving role beyond strategic consultations to tangible regional benefits.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
prelimsPoints: India participated in Quad Maritime Security Dialogue.
Emphasized free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific and rules-based order.
Discussed maritime domain awareness, counter-piracy, disaster relief.
Reiterated respect for UN Convention on the Law of the Sea (UNCLOS).
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic significance of the Quad in promoting a free and open Indo-Pacific and its implications for India's foreign policy and security interests.
Analyze the role of maritime security cooperation within the Quad framework in addressing regional challenges and upholding international law.
Evaluate the potential for the Quad to evolve into a more comprehensive mechanism for regional stability and development.
articles.imageDescription: Four national flags (India, US, Japan, Australia) arranged around a compass rose on a map of the Indo-Pacific.
articles.trivia: The Quad is an informal strategic dialogue between India, the United States, Japan, and Australia, maintained by semi-regular summits and information exchanges.

articles.headline: Government Launches Unified E-Governance Portal for Public Grievance Redressal
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances and Pensions launched a new unified e-governance portal designed to streamline and expedite public grievance redressal across all government departments.
• The portal provides a single window for citizens to lodge complaints, track their status in real-time, and receive timely resolutions, enhancing transparency and accountability.
• It integrates various existing grievance mechanisms into one platform, leveraging AI and machine learning for efficient routing and analysis of complaints.
• The initiative aims to improve citizen-centric governance, reduce bureaucratic delays, and foster greater trust between the public and government services.
• Training programs for government officials are being rolled out to ensure effective utilization of the new platform and responsive grievance handling.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Transparency and accountability and institutional and other measures
Role of Civil Services in a Democracy
prelimsPoints: Unified e-governance portal launched for public grievance redressal.
Provides single window for lodging and tracking complaints.
Integrates existing mechanisms, uses AI for routing.
Aims to enhance transparency and citizen-centric governance.
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of effective public grievance redressal mechanisms in a democratic setup and the role of e-governance in achieving it.
Analyze the features and potential impact of the new unified e-governance portal on transparency, accountability, and citizen satisfaction.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing such a large-scale digital initiative and suggest measures for its successful adoption and sustained effectiveness.
articles.imageDescription: A hand clicking on a 'Submit Grievance' button on a digital interface, with government buildings in the background.
articles.trivia: The concept of 'Right to Service' aims to provide citizens with a legal right to timely delivery of public services, with provisions for penalizing defaulting officials.

articles.headline: India's Solar Manufacturing Capacity Sees Record Boost with New PLI Scheme Investments
articles.explainer:
• India's solar photovoltaic (PV) manufacturing capacity witnessed an unprecedented surge, driven by fresh investments under the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for high-efficiency solar modules.
• Several major domestic and international players announced new fabrication units, aiming to reduce India's reliance on imported solar components and bolster energy security.
• The PLI scheme has successfully incentivized integrated manufacturing, covering the entire value chain from polysilicon to solar cells and modules.
• This expansion is crucial for achieving India's ambitious renewable energy targets and transitioning towards a green economy.
• The government expects the boost in local manufacturing to create thousands of jobs and establish India as a global hub for solar technology production.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Infrastructure: Energy
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Environmental pollution and degradation
prelimsPoints: India's solar manufacturing capacity boosted by PLI scheme investments.
Aims to reduce import reliance and bolster energy security.
PLI scheme incentivizes integrated manufacturing from polysilicon to modules.
Crucial for achieving renewable energy targets.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of boosting domestic solar manufacturing capacity for India's energy security and climate change mitigation goals.
Analyze the effectiveness of the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme in attracting investments and fostering an indigenous solar ecosystem.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in positioning India as a global leader in renewable energy manufacturing and technology.
articles.imageDescription: A large solar panel manufacturing facility with solar panels on the roof, symbolizing growth in renewable energy.
articles.trivia: India aims to achieve 500 GW of installed non-fossil energy capacity by 2030, with solar power being a major contributor.

highlights: India permits Iranian warship IRIS Lavan to dock in Kochi on humanitarian grounds amid regional conflict, showcasing strategic autonomy.
Supreme Court upholds extensive powers of Data Protection Authority, strengthening India's data governance and privacy framework.
Government launches 'Atmanirbhar Bharat 2.0' focusing on green manufacturing and export promotion in sustainable sectors.
ISRO successfully tests a next-generation Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) prototype, paving the way for cost-effective space access.
National Mission for Climate Resilient Agriculture (NMCRA) launched to safeguard food security against climate change impacts.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the recent docking of IRIS Lavan in Kochi, consider the following statements:
1. IRIS Lavan is a research vessel belonging to the Iranian Navy.
2. India allowed the docking on humanitarian grounds amidst the 2026 Iran war.
3. Kochi is a major port located on India's eastern coast.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: IRIS Lavan is an Iranian Navy vessel, but the context implies it's a warship or auxiliary vessel, not specifically a 'research vessel' in the provided source. Statement 2 is correct: The source explicitly mentions India allowed the docking on humanitarian grounds amid the 2026 Iran war. Statement 3 is incorrect: Kochi is located on India's southwestern coast, not eastern.

prelimsMocks.question:
The National Mission for Climate Resilient Agriculture (NMCRA) recently launched by the Indian government aims to achieve which of the following?
1. Promote drought-resistant crop varieties.
2. Develop precision irrigation techniques.
3. Integrate traditional farming practices with modern technology.
4. Establish a network of climate-resilient villages.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: All the statements are correct. The NMCRA aims to enhance agricultural resilience by focusing on drought-resistant crops, precision irrigation, integrating traditional and modern practices, and establishing climate-resilient villages to ensure long-term food security and improve farmers' livelihoods.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Reusable Launch Vehicle – Technology Demonstrator (RLV-TD) program of ISRO:
1. It aims to significantly reduce the cost of space launches.
2. The recent test involved a controlled landing experiment.
3. India is the first country globally to develop RLV technology.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The RLV program's primary goal is to drastically reduce the cost of space launches. Statement 2 is correct: The recent test involved a controlled landing experiment. Statement 3 is incorrect: While India is among a select few nations, it is not the first country globally to develop RLV technology; others like the USA (Space Shuttle, SpaceX) have pioneered or significantly advanced in this area.

prelimsMocks.question: With reference to the Data Protection Authority (DPA) in India, which of the following statements is correct?
options: (a) The DPA is a quasi-judicial body established under the Information Technology Act, 2000.
(b) The Supreme Court recently curtailed the DPA's powers to ensure a balance between privacy and state interests.
(c) The DPA's extensive enforcement powers have been upheld by the Supreme Court, strengthening India's data governance.
(d) The DPA primarily regulates data sharing between government agencies and is not concerned with private sector data handling.
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) The DPA's extensive enforcement powers have been upheld by the Supreme Court, strengthening India's data governance.
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement (c) is correct. The Supreme Court upheld the extensive enforcement powers of the DPA, which was established under the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023. Statement (a) is incorrect as it's under the DPDP Act, not IT Act 2000. Statement (b) is incorrect as the SC upheld, not curtailed, its powers. Statement (d) is incorrect as DPA regulates both public and private sector data handling.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is a non-permanent constitutional body established under Article 263 of the Indian Constitution to facilitate coordination between states and the Centre?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Inter-State Council is a non-permanent constitutional body established under Article 263 of the Indian Constitution. Its primary role is to inquire into and advise upon disputes between states, investigate and discuss subjects in which some or all of the states, or the Union and one or more of the states, have a common interest. NITI Aayog is an extra-constitutional body, Finance Commission is a constitutional body under Article 280, and Zonal Councils are statutory bodies.

mainsMocks.question: India's decision to allow the Iranian warship to dock in Kochi on humanitarian grounds, amidst regional conflict, reflects its evolving foreign policy approach. Discuss the principles guiding India's strategic autonomy in such complex geopolitical scenarios and its implications for India's role in the Indian Ocean Region. (150 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: India and its neighbourhood- relations; Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests; GS3: Security challenges and their management in border areas.
mainsMocks.approach: Define strategic autonomy and its relevance for India. Explain how the decision reflects humanitarian diplomacy and balancing interests. Discuss implications for India's maritime presence and regional stability, highlighting challenges and opportunities.

mainsMocks.question: The launch of 'Atmanirbhar Bharat 2.0' with a focus on green manufacturing and export promotion signifies a strategic shift in India's economic policy. Analyze how this initiative can contribute to sustainable economic growth, job creation, and India's global competitiveness while addressing environmental concerns. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment; Inclusive growth and issues arising from it; Government Budgeting; Environmental pollution and degradation.
mainsMocks.approach: Briefly introduce Atmanirbhar Bharat 2.0 and its green focus. Discuss its potential for sustainable growth (decoupling growth from emissions), job creation in green sectors, and enhancing global competitiveness (green exports). Address how it tackles environmental concerns and aligns with climate goals. Conclude with challenges and way forward.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's upholding of the Data Protection Authority's powers is a landmark decision for India's digital governance. Examine the significance of this ruling for individual privacy, corporate accountability, and the future of India's digital economy. (150 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors; Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies; Right to Privacy; GS3: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges.
mainsMocks.approach: Explain the context of the DPDP Act and DPA. Discuss the significance for individual privacy (stronger rights, data control), corporate accountability (compliance, penalties), and the digital economy (trust, global standards, innovation vs. regulation). Conclude on the balance struck and future implications.

quickBytes.text: The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, establishes the Data Protection Authority (DPA).
Atmanirbhar Bharat Abhiyan, launched in 2020, aims for India's self-reliance across sectors.
ISRO's RLV-TD (Reusable Launch Vehicle – Technology Demonstrator) aims to reduce launch costs.
The Ramsar Convention is an international treaty for the conservation and wise use of wetlands.
Article 263 of the Indian Constitution provides for the establishment of the Inter-State Council.
Neglected Tropical Diseases (NTDs) affect over one billion people in tropical and subtropical regions.
The 'Act East Policy' is India's diplomatic initiative to promote economic, strategic and cultural relations with the Indo-Pacific region.
The UN Convention on the Law of the Sea (UNCLOS) is the international treaty governing maritime activities.
India aims to achieve 500 GW of installed non-fossil energy capacity by 2030.
Hybrid threats combine conventional, irregular, cyber, and information warfare tactics.

GS1.headline: UNESCO Recognizes Traditional Indian Textile Craft as Intangible Cultural Heritage
GS1.explainer:
• The United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization (UNESCO) officially inscribed a prominent traditional Indian textile craft onto its Representative List of the Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity.
• This recognition highlights the intricate artistry, historical significance, and community-based knowledge systems associated with the craft, which has been passed down through generations.
• The designation is expected to provide a significant boost to the artisans involved, promoting their livelihoods, and attracting global attention to India's rich cultural heritage.
• It also underscores the urgent need for preservation efforts against modernization pressures and challenges in sustaining traditional skills.
• India's Ministry of Culture expressed commitment to developing comprehensive strategies for safeguarding and promoting this newly recognized heritage, including skill development and market linkages.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Salient features of Indian Society, Diversity of India.
prelimsPoints: UNESCO recognized a traditional Indian textile craft as Intangible Cultural Heritage.
Aims to boost artisans' livelihoods and promote cultural preservation.
Highlights intricate artistry and historical significance.
India has several elements on UNESCO's Intangible Cultural Heritage list.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of UNESCO's recognition of traditional Indian crafts for cultural preservation, economic development, and India's soft power.
Analyze the challenges faced by traditional artisans in India and propose strategies for sustaining and promoting intangible cultural heritage.
Evaluate the role of government policies and community initiatives in safeguarding traditional art forms against modernization and commercialization.
GS1.imageDescription: A close-up of intricate traditional Indian textile patterns being woven by an artisan's hands.
GS1.trivia: India has numerous elements on UNESCO's Intangible Cultural Heritage list, including Yoga, Kumbh Mela, and Novruz.

GS2.headline: India Allows Iranian Warship IRIS Lavan to Dock in Kochi on Humanitarian Grounds Amid Regional Tensions
GS2.explainer:
• The Indian government granted permission for the Iranian Navy vessel IRIS Lavan to dock at Kochi port, citing humanitarian grounds during the ongoing 2026 Iran war.
• This decision underscores India's commitment to humanitarian principles and its historically neutral stance in regional conflicts, despite geopolitical complexities.
• The move highlights India's strategic autonomy in foreign policy, balancing international relations with its national interests and maritime security concerns.
• The docking facilitates necessary repairs and replenishment for the vessel, which was reportedly facing distress, without implying political endorsement of any party in the conflict.
• The incident brings into focus India's role as a responsible maritime power and a provider of humanitarian assistance in the Indian Ocean region.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
prelimsPoints: IRIS Lavan is an Iranian Navy vessel.
Kochi is a major port on India's southwestern coast.
The docking was allowed on 'humanitarian grounds'.
Context is the '2026 Iran war'.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's balanced approach to regional conflicts and its commitment to humanitarian diplomacy.
Analyze the implications of such decisions on India's strategic autonomy and its relations with other global powers.
Evaluate India's role as a net security provider and humanitarian responder in the Indian Ocean Region.
Examine the challenges of maintaining neutrality amidst evolving geopolitical landscapes.
GS2.imageDescription: An illustration of an Iranian naval vessel docked at a port, with the Indian coastline in the background.
GS2.trivia: Kochi, historically known as Cochin, was a pivotal spice trade center and has been nicknamed the 'Queen of the Arabian Sea'.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: International Relations
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Impact of global events on India

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Data Protection Authority's Powers in Landmark Privacy Ruling
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India delivered a crucial verdict, affirming the extensive enforcement powers of the Data Protection Authority (DPA) established under the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023.
• The ruling validates DPA's authority to impose penalties, conduct investigations, and issue directives, significantly strengthening India's data governance and privacy framework.
• The court emphasized the importance of balancing individual right to privacy with legitimate state interests and the needs of a digital economy, setting a precedent for future data regulation.
• This judgment will have far-reaching implications for technology companies operating in India, mandating stricter compliance with data handling and processing norms.
• Experts suggest the ruling bolsters India's position as a robust regulatory environment for digital services, potentially influencing global data protection standards.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies
Right to Privacy
Information Technology
keyTerms: Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act)
prelimsPoints: The Supreme Court upheld the powers of the Data Protection Authority (DPA).
The DPA was established under the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023.
The ruling pertains to individual right to privacy and data governance.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the Supreme Court's ruling on the DPA's powers for India's digital economy and individual rights.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in implementing a robust data protection regime in a rapidly digitizing society.
Evaluate the role of statutory bodies like the DPA in upholding constitutional principles and ensuring fair digital practices.
GS2.imageDescription: A gavel resting on law books with binary code flowing in the background, symbolizing digital justice.
GS2.trivia: India's Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, is one of the most comprehensive data protection laws globally, drawing parallels with the EU's GDPR.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies

GS2.headline: India-ASEAN Summit Concludes with Focus on Digital Economy and Maritime Security Cooperation
GS2.explainer:
• The annual India-ASEAN Summit concluded with significant agreements aimed at deepening cooperation in the digital economy and strengthening maritime security across the Indo-Pacific.
• Leaders endorsed a 'Digital Partnership Framework' to facilitate cross-border digital payments, data sharing protocols, and collaboration on emerging technologies like AI and blockchain.
• In maritime security, joint declarations emphasized adherence to international law, freedom of navigation, and enhanced cooperation in counter-piracy, search and rescue, and disaster relief operations.
• The summit reinforced India's 'Act East Policy', highlighting ASEAN as a central pillar in India's vision for a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific region.
• Discussions also covered regional connectivity projects, trade facilitation, and collective efforts to address climate change and sustainable development goals.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
India and its neighbourhood- relations
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India-ASEAN Summit focused on digital economy and maritime security.
A 'Digital Partnership Framework' was endorsed.
Commitment to freedom of navigation and international law in Indo-Pacific was reiterated.
Reinforces India's 'Act East Policy'.
mainsPoints: Examine the significance of India-ASEAN cooperation in shaping the geopolitical and geoeconomic landscape of the Indo-Pacific region.
Discuss how the 'Digital Partnership Framework' can enhance regional economic integration and digital governance.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in strengthening maritime security cooperation between India and ASEAN member states.
GS2.imageDescription: A handshake between representatives of India and ASEAN nations, with digital network lines and ships in the background.
GS2.trivia: ASEAN (Association of Southeast Asian Nations) was founded in 1967 and comprises ten member states in Southeast Asia.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: International Relations
prelimsSyllabus.area: Important International institutions, agencies and fora

GS2.headline: National Health Mission Expands Telemedicine Services to Remote Rural Areas
GS2.explainer:
• The National Health Mission (NHM) announced a significant expansion of its telemedicine services, aiming to reach the most remote and underserved rural populations across India.
• This initiative leverages India's growing digital infrastructure, including widespread internet connectivity and mobile penetration, to provide remote medical consultations, diagnostics, and specialist referrals.
• The expansion seeks to bridge the critical gap in healthcare access, particularly addressing the shortage of doctors and medical facilities in rural areas.
• Training programs for community health workers (ASHAs and ANMs) are being intensified to facilitate teleconsultations and ensure effective last-mile delivery of healthcare.
• The move is expected to improve health outcomes, reduce out-of-pocket expenditure for rural families, and enhance the overall efficiency of the public health system.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: National Health Mission (NHM) expanded telemedicine services to rural areas.
Aims to bridge healthcare access gap and address doctor shortage.
Leverages digital infrastructure and trains community health workers.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of telemedicine in achieving universal healthcare access and equity in India, especially for rural populations.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities associated with scaling up digital health services in a diverse country like India.
Evaluate how the expansion of NHM's telemedicine services aligns with the broader goals of Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission.
GS2.imageDescription: A doctor on a video call with a patient in a rural setting, symbolizing telemedicine bridging geographical gaps.
GS2.trivia: The National Health Mission (NHM) was launched in 2013, subsuming the National Rural Health Mission (NRHM) and the National Urban Health Mission (NUHM).

GS2.headline: Inter-State Council Meeting Discusses Fiscal Federalism and Cooperative Development
GS2.explainer:
• The Inter-State Council convened a crucial meeting, bringing together Chief Ministers and Union Ministers to deliberate on matters pertaining to fiscal federalism and cooperative development.
• Discussions focused on equitable revenue sharing mechanisms, devolution of funds to states, and enhancing financial autonomy for state governments to foster balanced regional growth.
• The Council emphasized the need for greater collaboration between the Centre and States on key developmental projects, especially in critical sectors like infrastructure, education, and healthcare.
• Proposals for streamlining inter-state trade and investment policies were also considered, aiming to create a more unified national market.
• The meeting reinforced the spirit of cooperative federalism, seeking to resolve inter-state disputes and strengthen the institutional framework for Centre-State relations.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
prelimsPoints: Inter-State Council meeting discussed fiscal federalism and cooperative development.
Focused on revenue sharing, devolution of funds, and state financial autonomy.
Aimed at strengthening Centre-State relations and resolving disputes.
The Inter-State Council is established under Article 263 of the Constitution.
mainsPoints: Discuss the concept of fiscal federalism in India and the challenges in achieving equitable resource distribution between the Centre and States.
Analyze the role and significance of the Inter-State Council in promoting cooperative federalism and resolving inter-state disputes.
Suggest reforms to strengthen the institutional mechanisms for Centre-State cooperation and improve financial autonomy of states.
GS2.imageDescription: A map of India with interconnected lines, symbolizing Centre-State cooperation, with government officials in a meeting setting.
GS2.trivia: The Inter-State Council is a non-permanent constitutional body established under Article 263 of the Indian Constitution to facilitate coordination between states and the Centre.

GS2.headline: India Participates in Quad Maritime Security Dialogue, Emphasizing Rules-Based Order
GS2.explainer:
• India participated in a high-level Quad Maritime Security Dialogue, reaffirming its commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific region based on a rules-based international order.
• Representatives from India, the United States, Japan, and Australia discussed enhanced cooperation in maritime domain awareness, counter-piracy operations, and disaster relief efforts.
• The dialogue focused on strengthening interoperability among member navies and sharing best practices for maintaining stability and security in critical sea lanes.
• India reiterated its stance on respecting international law, particularly the UN Convention on the Law of the Sea (UNCLOS), as the foundation for maritime governance.
• The Quad's growing focus on practical cooperation in maritime security underscores its evolving role beyond strategic consultations to tangible regional benefits.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
prelimsPoints: India participated in Quad Maritime Security Dialogue.
Emphasized free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific and rules-based order.
Discussed maritime domain awareness, counter-piracy, disaster relief.
Reiterated respect for UN Convention on the Law of the Sea (UNCLOS).
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic significance of the Quad in promoting a free and open Indo-Pacific and its implications for India's foreign policy and security interests.
Analyze the role of maritime security cooperation within the Quad framework in addressing regional challenges and upholding international law.
Evaluate the potential for the Quad to evolve into a more comprehensive mechanism for regional stability and development.
GS2.imageDescription: Four national flags (India, US, Japan, Australia) arranged around a compass rose on a map of the Indo-Pacific.
GS2.trivia: The Quad is an informal strategic dialogue between India, the United States, Japan, and Australia, maintained by semi-regular summits and information exchanges.

GS2.headline: Government Launches Unified E-Governance Portal for Public Grievance Redressal
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances and Pensions launched a new unified e-governance portal designed to streamline and expedite public grievance redressal across all government departments.
• The portal provides a single window for citizens to lodge complaints, track their status in real-time, and receive timely resolutions, enhancing transparency and accountability.
• It integrates various existing grievance mechanisms into one platform, leveraging AI and machine learning for efficient routing and analysis of complaints.
• The initiative aims to improve citizen-centric governance, reduce bureaucratic delays, and foster greater trust between the public and government services.
• Training programs for government officials are being rolled out to ensure effective utilization of the new platform and responsive grievance handling.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Transparency and accountability and institutional and other measures
Role of Civil Services in a Democracy
prelimsPoints: Unified e-governance portal launched for public grievance redressal.
Provides single window for lodging and tracking complaints.
Integrates existing mechanisms, uses AI for routing.
Aims to enhance transparency and citizen-centric governance.
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of effective public grievance redressal mechanisms in a democratic setup and the role of e-governance in achieving it.
Analyze the features and potential impact of the new unified e-governance portal on transparency, accountability, and citizen satisfaction.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing such a large-scale digital initiative and suggest measures for its successful adoption and sustained effectiveness.
GS2.imageDescription: A hand clicking on a 'Submit Grievance' button on a digital interface, with government buildings in the background.
GS2.trivia: The concept of 'Right to Service' aims to provide citizens with a legal right to timely delivery of public services, with provisions for penalizing defaulting officials.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils 'Atmanirbhar Bharat 2.0' with Focus on Green Manufacturing and Export Boost
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Government launched the second phase of the 'Atmanirbhar Bharat Abhiyan', shifting its focus towards sustainable and green manufacturing practices.
• Key initiatives include substantial incentives for industries adopting renewable energy sources, producing electric vehicle components, and engaging in circular economy models.
• The policy aims to boost India's export capabilities in emerging sectors like green technology, advanced materials, and sustainable agriculture products.
• It seeks to create a resilient global supply chain, positioning India as a reliable manufacturing hub for environmentally conscious products.
• This phase emphasizes skill development and R&D in green technologies to foster innovation and create new employment opportunities.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it
Government Budgeting
Environmental pollution and degradation
prelimsPoints: 'Atmanirbhar Bharat 2.0' focuses on green manufacturing and export boost.
Incentives target renewable energy, EV components, and circular economy models.
Aims to position India as a sustainable manufacturing hub.
mainsPoints: Analyze the potential impact of 'Atmanirbhar Bharat 2.0' on India's economic growth, environmental sustainability, and global trade position.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in transitioning towards a green manufacturing economy in India.
Evaluate how this policy aligns with India's international climate commitments and sustainable development goals.
GS3.imageDescription: A factory emitting green smoke, symbolizing eco-friendly industrial growth, with the Indian flag subtly in the background.
GS3.trivia: The original Atmanirbhar Bharat Abhiyan, launched in 2020, was a comprehensive economic package aimed at making India self-reliant across various sectors.

GS3.headline: India Launches National Mission for Climate Resilient Agriculture (NMCRA)
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare officially launched the National Mission for Climate Resilient Agriculture (NMCRA), a flagship program to combat climate change impacts on farming.
• The mission focuses on developing and deploying climate-smart agricultural practices, including drought-resistant crop varieties, precision irrigation techniques, and soil health management.
• It aims to integrate traditional ecological knowledge with modern agricultural science to enhance the adaptive capacity of Indian farmers.
• NMCRA will establish a network of climate-resilient villages and promote sustainable land and water resource management across vulnerable regions.
• The initiative is crucial for ensuring long-term food security and improving farmers' livelihoods amidst increasing climate variability.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country
Storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints
Food processing and related industries in India
prelimsPoints: NMCRA stands for National Mission for Climate Resilient Agriculture.
It aims to combat climate change impacts on farming.
Focus areas include drought-resistant crops, precision irrigation, and soil health management.
mainsPoints: Discuss the necessity and strategic objectives of the National Mission for Climate Resilient Agriculture (NMCRA) in the context of India's agricultural challenges.
Analyze how NMCRA can contribute to food security, farmers' income, and environmental sustainability.
Evaluate the potential for integrating traditional farming wisdom with modern scientific approaches under this mission.
GS3.imageDescription: A farmer working in a field with advanced irrigation systems and changing weather patterns in the sky, symbolizing climate resilience.
GS3.trivia: India is highly vulnerable to climate change impacts on agriculture, with millions of farmers dependent on monsoon rains.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Prototype, Advancing Space Access
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully conducted a crucial test flight of its Reusable Launch Vehicle – Technology Demonstrator (RLV-TD) prototype, marking a significant milestone.
• This test involved a controlled landing experiment simulating the return of a space vehicle to Earth, a critical step towards developing a fully reusable two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) RLV.
• The successful demonstration aims to drastically reduce the cost of space launches, making space access more affordable and frequent for commercial and scientific missions.
• The RLV program is central to ISRO's long-term strategy for future space exploration, including human spaceflight and setting up space stations.
• India's indigenous development of RLV technology positions it among a select few nations with such advanced capabilities, boosting its competitiveness in the global space market.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Space
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: ISRO successfully tested its Reusable Launch Vehicle – Technology Demonstrator (RLV-TD) prototype.
The test involved a controlled landing experiment.
The RLV program aims to reduce space launch costs.
India is among a few nations developing RLV technology.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) program for India's future in space exploration and the global space market.
Analyze how cost-effective space access through RLV technology can democratize space and foster innovation.
Evaluate the technological challenges and breakthroughs involved in developing indigenous RLV capabilities.
GS3.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of ISRO's RLV-TD descending through the atmosphere towards a landing strip.
GS3.trivia: ISRO's RLV-TD program is similar in concept to NASA's Space Shuttle and SpaceX's Falcon 9 reusable rockets, aiming for significant cost reductions.

GS3.headline: New Counter-Terrorism Policy Framework Announced to Combat Hybrid Threats
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Home Affairs unveiled a comprehensive new Counter-Terrorism Policy Framework, specifically designed to address the evolving nature of 'hybrid threats'.
• The policy emphasizes enhanced intelligence gathering and sharing mechanisms, both domestically and internationally, to preempt and neutralize terror plots.
• It incorporates strategies to counter threats posed by cyber-terrorism, drone attacks, lone-wolf actors, and the online radicalization of youth.
• A key focus is on strengthening inter-agency coordination between various security forces, intelligence agencies, and state police departments.
• The framework also includes provisions for community engagement and de-radicalization programs to build societal resilience against extremist ideologies.
gsSubTopics: Linkages between development and spread of extremism
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security
Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
prelimsPoints: New Counter-Terrorism Policy Framework addresses 'hybrid threats'.
Focuses on cyber-terrorism, drone attacks, and online radicalization.
Emphasizes enhanced intelligence sharing and inter-agency coordination.
Includes community engagement and de-radicalization programs.
mainsPoints: Discuss the changing nature of terrorism and the emergence of 'hybrid threats' in India's internal security landscape.
Analyze the key features and strategic objectives of the new Counter-Terrorism Policy Framework in addressing these contemporary challenges.
Evaluate the effectiveness of multi-pronged approaches, including technological solutions, intelligence, and community engagement, in combating terrorism.
GS3.imageDescription: A stylized graphic showing interconnected threats like drones, cyber symbols, and radicalization, converging on a map of India.
GS3.trivia: Hybrid threats combine conventional, irregular, cyber, and information warfare tactics, often blurring the lines between state and non-state actors.

GS3.headline: Major Boost to National Logistics Policy with New Integrated Freight Corridors
GS3.explainer:
• The government announced a significant expansion of the National Logistics Policy (NLP) with the sanctioning of several new integrated freight corridors across key economic regions.
• These corridors aim to seamlessly connect manufacturing hubs, consumption centers, and ports through dedicated rail, road, and waterway networks, drastically improving freight movement efficiency.
• The initiative is projected to reduce logistics costs from the current high percentage of GDP, making Indian goods more competitive in domestic and international markets.
• It integrates advanced technologies like IoT, AI, and blockchain for real-time tracking, optimized route planning, and predictive maintenance of logistics infrastructure.
• The development is expected to create numerous employment opportunities, attract private investment in infrastructure, and accelerate economic growth.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Investment models
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
prelimsPoints: National Logistics Policy (NLP) boosted with new integrated freight corridors.
Aims to connect manufacturing hubs, consumption centers, and ports.
Expected to reduce logistics costs and improve competitiveness.
Integrates IoT, AI, and blockchain for logistics management.
mainsPoints: Discuss the critical role of robust logistics infrastructure in achieving India's economic growth and manufacturing aspirations.
Analyze how the new integrated freight corridors under NLP can address existing challenges in supply chain efficiency and cost reduction.
Evaluate the potential socio-economic benefits and environmental considerations of large-scale infrastructure projects like freight corridors.
GS3.imageDescription: An aerial view of a complex network of railways, roads, and waterways converging, symbolizing integrated freight corridors.
GS3.trivia: Logistics costs in India are significantly higher than global benchmarks, making improvements critical for boosting manufacturing competitiveness.

GS3.headline: Ministry of Environment Designates Five New Ramsar Sites, Boosting Wetland Conservation Efforts
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change announced the addition of five new sites to India's list of Wetlands of International Importance under the Ramsar Convention.
• This designation significantly boosts India's efforts in wetland conservation, recognizing the ecological importance of these sites for biodiversity, water regulation, and local livelihoods.
• The newly added sites span diverse geographical regions and include a mix of natural and man-made wetlands, each supporting unique flora and fauna.
• The Ramsar Convention provides a framework for national action and international cooperation for the conservation and wise use of wetlands and their resources.
• The move emphasizes the need for integrated management plans to protect these fragile ecosystems from pollution, encroachment, and climate change impacts.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
Environmental impact assessment
prelimsPoints: Five new Ramsar Sites were designated in India.
Ramsar Convention is an international treaty for wetland conservation.
Wetlands are crucial for biodiversity, water regulation, and livelihoods.
The new sites cover diverse geographical regions.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological and socio-economic significance of wetlands and the importance of the Ramsar Convention in their conservation.
Analyze the challenges faced in wetland management in India and how the designation of new Ramsar sites contributes to addressing these challenges.
Suggest measures for effective implementation of wetland conservation strategies, including community participation and policy frameworks.
GS3.imageDescription: A serene wetland landscape with various migratory birds, symbolizing biodiversity and conservation.
GS3.trivia: The Ramsar Convention was signed in Ramsar, Iran, in 1971, making it one of the oldest intergovernmental environmental agreements.

GS3.headline: Breakthrough in AI-Powered Drug Discovery for Neglected Tropical Diseases
GS3.explainer:
• Indian research institutions, led by the Council of Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR), announced a significant breakthrough in using Artificial Intelligence (AI) for drug discovery.
• Researchers successfully identified several promising drug candidates for neglected tropical diseases (NTDs) by employing advanced AI algorithms to screen millions of compounds.
• This AI-driven approach drastically reduces the time and cost traditionally associated with drug development, offering hope for affordable treatments for diseases affecting vulnerable populations.
• The initiative highlights India's growing capabilities in leveraging cutting-edge technology for public health challenges and global health equity.
• Future plans include clinical trials for the identified compounds and collaboration with international pharmaceutical companies to accelerate the development and distribution of new drugs.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Health
prelimsPoints: Indian institutions achieved breakthrough in AI-powered drug discovery for Neglected Tropical Diseases (NTDs).
AI algorithms screened millions of compounds to identify drug candidates.
Aims to reduce time and cost of drug development.
CSIR is involved in this research.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of Artificial Intelligence in accelerating drug discovery and addressing global health challenges, particularly for neglected diseases.
Analyze how India's advancements in AI and biotechnology can contribute to global health equity and affordable healthcare solutions.
