Evaluate the ethical considerations and regulatory frameworks required for AI-driven drug development and deployment.
GS3.imageDescription: A stylized image of a human brain merging with a molecular structure, symbolizing AI's role in drug discovery.
GS3.trivia: Neglected Tropical Diseases (NTDs) are a diverse group of communicable diseases that prevail in tropical and subtropical conditions in 149 countries and affect more than one billion people.

GS3.headline: RBI Introduces New Regulatory Framework for Digital Banks and Fintech Lenders
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) unveiled a new comprehensive regulatory framework aimed at governing digital banks and other fintech lending platforms.
• The framework seeks to ensure financial stability, protect consumer interests, and promote responsible innovation in the rapidly expanding digital lending space.
• Key provisions include stricter capital adequacy norms for digital banks, enhanced data privacy requirements, and robust grievance redressal mechanisms for customers.
• The move is designed to mitigate risks associated with predatory lending practices, data breaches, and systemic vulnerabilities that could arise from unregulated digital financial services.
• It is expected to create a level playing field for traditional banks and fintech entities, fostering healthy competition while ensuring financial inclusion and market integrity.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it
Government Budgeting
Financial market
prelimsPoints: RBI introduced a new regulatory framework for digital banks and fintech lenders.
Aims for financial stability, consumer protection, and responsible innovation.
Includes capital adequacy norms, data privacy, and grievance redressal mechanisms.
Seeks to mitigate risks from predatory lending and data breaches.
mainsPoints: Discuss the rationale behind RBI's new regulatory framework for digital banks and fintech lenders in the context of India's evolving financial landscape.
Analyze the potential benefits and challenges of this framework for financial inclusion, innovation, and consumer protection.
Evaluate how a balanced regulatory approach can foster growth in the digital financial sector while safeguarding systemic stability.
GS3.imageDescription: A digital padlock over a stack of coins and credit cards, symbolizing financial regulation in the digital age.
GS3.trivia: The RBI had previously issued guidelines for digital lending in 2022, but this new framework is more comprehensive, specifically addressing digital banks.

GS3.headline: India's Solar Manufacturing Capacity Sees Record Boost with New PLI Scheme Investments
GS3.explainer:
• India's solar photovoltaic (PV) manufacturing capacity witnessed an unprecedented surge, driven by fresh investments under the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for high-efficiency solar modules.
• Several major domestic and international players announced new fabrication units, aiming to reduce India's reliance on imported solar components and bolster energy security.
• The PLI scheme has successfully incentivized integrated manufacturing, covering the entire value chain from polysilicon to solar cells and modules.
• This expansion is crucial for achieving India's ambitious renewable energy targets and transitioning towards a green economy.
• The government expects the boost in local manufacturing to create thousands of jobs and establish India as a global hub for solar technology production.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment

PLI scheme incentivizes integrated manufacturing from polysilicon to modules.

Analyze the effectiveness of the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme in attracting investments and fostering an indigenous solar ecosystem.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in positioning India as a global leader in renewable energy manufacturing and technology.
GS3.imageDescription: A large solar panel manufacturing facility with solar panels on the roof, symbolizing growth in renewable energy.
GS3.trivia: India aims to achieve 500 GW of installed non-fossil energy capacity by 2030, with solar power being a major contributor.
